chunks.text: Order Sheet IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No.S-174 of 2025 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge. Hearing of Case 1.For orders on office objections 2.For hearing of main case 27-08-2025 Mr. Sikandar Sadar Siddiqui, Advocate for the petitioners. M/s Zulfiqar Ali Jatoi, Additional P.G and Saeed Ahmed Wassan, Assistant A.G a/w ASI Saddam Hussain Khokhar, of P.S, Moro. **** Mr. Fazal Muhammad Bhutto, Advocate files Vakalatnama on behalf of private respondent No.7. Learned counsel for petitioners also places on record certain documents under the cover of his statement. Learned Law Officers file statements on behalf of official respondent No.5, which are taken on record. Learned counsel for respondent No.7 submits that in fact a civil dispute exists between the parties over landed property, and in this regard F.C. Suit No.188 of 2024 has been instituted by respondent No.7 against the petitioners, which is pending adjudication before the Court of Senior Civil Judge, Moro. Learned counsel for the petitioners submits that two counter FIRs have been lodged, one by respondent No.7 and other by petitioner No.1, bearing FIRs No.72 of 2025 and 250 of 2025 respectively at Police Station Moro, against each other. Learned Law Officers submit that the official respondents shall act strictly in accordance with law and ensure that no harassment shall be caused to the petitioners. With regard to the aforesaid FIRs, it is submitted that investigation in both cases is still in progress. In view of the above statements, learned counsel for the petitioners, being satisfied, seeks disposal of the petition.

With regard to the aforesaid FIRs, it is submitted that investigation in both cases is still in progress. In view of the above statements, learned counsel for the petitioners, being satisfied, seeks disposal of the petition. 2 2 Accordingly, the instant petition stands disposed of with directions to the official respondents to act strictly in accordance with law and to ensure that no harassment is caused to the petitioners. As regards the criminal cases and civil suit, both parties are directed to pursue their remedies before the competent forums/Courts of law. However, it is made clear that this order shall not come in the way of any criminal and/or civil proceedings if pending between the parties before the competent Courts of law. JUDGE Ahmad
